query: Angular Zone.js under the hood: async patching, stale-view debug, and zoneless trade-offs

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this Angular, RxJS, or NgRx prompt, so broader Angular question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: Angular Zone Pollution
competitors.selectionReason: This Angular best-practices page is the most direct official source explaining that Zone.js signals Angular after async work and why that affects change detection.
theirStrengths: It directly explains Zone.js as the signaling mechanism Angular uses to schedule change detection and shows runOutsideAngular patterns.
theirGaps: It is optimization-focused and less shaped as a compact interview answer about why the view updated after async work.

realWorldUseCases:
Async trigger model

Zone.js is Angular's classic answer to "why did the view update after that async work?" It patches timers, promises, DOM events, and similar browser APIs, then gives Angular a hook to run change detection after the task completes. That model matters when you debug a stale view, wire third-party callbacks, or move part of the app to zoneless change detection.
followUpConfusion:
Boundary rule
  • Use runOutsideAngular for noisy work like scroll listeners or animations that should not trigger every check.
  • Zoneless apps remove the automatic async hook, so you must understand where updates are scheduled.
  • Most Zone.js bugs are really ownership bugs: which async boundary told Angular to check?

realWorldUseCases: The competitor covers the core concept, but our page is now more explicit about the production decision or failure mode behind this topic.
actionableExamples: The competitor still provides competitive examples, so the example bar remains tied instead of a clean sweep.
followUpConfusion: Our updated page now answers the likely follow-up confusion more directly with a sharper rule, boundary, or failure pattern.
